methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Athanor operates in Robotics & Manufacturing at the stealth stage. The comparable universe consists of one public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. The sector contains 11 total tracked companies, though the stealth stage and company-specific profile limit direct peer overlap.

Peer-median valuation proved inapplicable. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector yielded insufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similarly-tiered comparables—zero datapoints met the minimum threshold of three for statistical reliability. A broad sector median would introduce unacceptable selection bias given the heterogeneity of robotics subsegments, manufacturing verticals, and business models represented across the peer set. No triangulated valuation could therefore be supported through this method.

Without sufficient stage-matched or tier-matched peers and absent disclosed financial metrics for the target, a defensible valuation range cannot be derived from the available data. Key uncertainties include Athanor's specific subsector focus within robotics, revenue or unit economics relative to named peers, and the degree of technology maturation. Future valuation work requires either identification of additional stealth-stage robotics comparables with disclosed terms, or disclosure of Athanor's own traction metrics to enable revenue or growth-multiple approaches.
